notes: The engineering layer keeps Shankara's reading (sāmarthyam earned in prior battles, the specific identity-stake of the warrior whose prowess is being denigrated) distinct from Ramanuja's reading (the specific kind of slander to which a hero cannot publicly respond, in the only forum where the rewriting carries weight). Both readings appear in the source pack's commentaries section labelled by tradition (Advaita, Vishishtadvaita, Kumara Sampradaya, Gaudiya Vaishnava). The engineering layer adopts both readings as complementary — earned identity (Shankara) + specific narrating forum (Ramanuja) — rather than collapsing them. The verse's place in the disgrace-argument sequence (2.34-2.36) is named structurally; the rhetorical-climax function of 'tato duḥkhataraṁ nu kim' is preserved as 'no greater pain than this for someone whose identity is the work.' The kṣatriya-honor frame is not flattened into general professional pride; the engineering layer explicitly localizes the pain to 'someone whose identity is engineering capability,' which is the structural analog of the warrior-class identity stake. PASS.
